name: Human Circulatory System Dual-Loop Diagram with Vessel Cross-Sections and Oxygen Data
description:
Create a 4-page ActivInspire flipchart for a biology lesson on the circulatory system.

Page 1: Title "The Circulatory System: Pulmonary & Systemic Loops" with subtitle "Blood Flow and Oxygen Transport".

Page 2 - Dual-Loop Flow Diagram: Three rectangles arranged vertically — "LUNGS" at top, "HEART" at center, "BODY TISSUES" at bottom. A blue upward arrow labeled "Pulmonary Artery - Deoxygenated" and a red downward arrow labeled "Pulmonary Vein - Oxygenated" connect HEART and LUNGS. A red downward arrow labeled "Aorta - Oxygenated" and a blue upward arrow labeled "Vena Cava - Deoxygenated" connect HEART and BODY TISSUES. A green circle labeled "Gas Exchange" beside both LUNGS and BODY TISSUES. The upper circuit labeled "Pulmonary Loop", the lower "Systemic Loop".

Page 3 - Blood Vessel Cross-Sections: Three side-by-side vessel cross-sections as concentric circles. Artery: large red-filled circle with smaller white circle inside (thick wall, small lumen). Vein: large blue-filled circle with larger white circle inside (thin wall, large lumen). Capillary: small circle with thin outline (one cell thick). Each labeled with vessel name and key characteristic.

Page 4 - Comparison Table: Title "Blood Vessel Oxygen & Pressure Data". A table with headers: Vessel, O2 (%), Pressure (mmHg), Direction. Rows: Pulmonary Artery (75, 25, Heart to Lungs), Pulmonary Vein (100, 8, Lungs to Heart), Aorta (98, 120, Heart to Body), Vena Cava (75, 5, Body to Heart).

Save as circulatory_system_lesson.flipchart in C:\Users\Docker\Documents\Flipcharts\.
